title: Nebraska Public Records Request - State Generative AI Records

communications.subject: Nebraska Public Records Act Request: Nebraska Public Records Request - State Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nebraska Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

I request records related to Nebraska state government use and governance of generative AI tools, including tools discussed under NITC AI policy section 8-609 such as ChatGPT, Copilot, Gemini, and related products.

We are requesting the following records:
1. All interaction logs, prompts, inputs, outputs, conversation histories, and similar records created by employees using ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, Adobe AI Assistant, and any other generative AI chatbot tools used by Nebraska state employees for the period from November 8, 2024 through the date this request is processed. Please provide these records in their native electronic format where available, including HTML exports, JSON, CSV, ZIP exports, or equivalent structured formats.
2. Any administrative usage logs, dashboards, reports, or other records sufficient to show which employees, offices, or units had access to these tools and the volume of usage during the same period.
3. Any policies, guidelines, training materials, approval documents, acceptable-use rules, or governance materials governing employee use of these tools or any other generative AI chatbot tools.
4. Any records retention, deletion, preservation, or disposition policies applicable to these interaction logs or related AI-generated records.

To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data, which generates a downloadable ZIP file containing conversations in HTML format. For Claude, export is similar, except via “Settings > Privacy > Export Data”. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console, the Purview compliance center, or individual user account settings. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/ (reporting on AI-generated government documents obtained through public records requests in Everett, WA); see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files (collecting records of AI usage at the Texas Department of Transportation released in response to a public records request).

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

If you contend that any portion of the requested records is exempt from disclosure, please identify the specific legal basis for the withholding and release all reasonably segregable non-exempt material. If any part of this request is unclear, I am happy to discuss ways to clarify or narrow it while preserving the core records sought.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jacqueline Xiong

title: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request - St. Paul AI Records

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request - St. Paul AI Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

I request records related to the City of Saint Paul's employee use of AI tools, including Axon Auto-Transcribe and records governed by the St. Paul Police Department's Generative AI Policy 236.06.

We are requesting the following records:
1. All interaction logs, prompts, inputs, outputs, conversation histories, and similar records created by employees using Axon Auto-Transcribe, any department-approved generative AI tools, and any other employee-facing AI chatbot or AI-assisted drafting tools used by City of Saint Paul personnel for the period from October 17, 2025 through the date this request is processed. Please provide these records in their native electronic format where available, including HTML exports, JSON, CSV, ZIP exports, or equivalent structured formats.
2. Any administrative usage logs, dashboards, reports, or other records sufficient to show which employees, offices, or units had access to these tools and the volume of usage during the same period.
3. Any policies, guidelines, training materials, approval documents, acceptable-use rules, or governance materials governing employee use of these tools or any other generative AI chatbot tools.
4. Any records retention, deletion, preservation, or disposition policies applicable to these interaction logs or related AI-generated records.
5. Any records showing whether Draft One or any similar AI report-writing tools were evaluated, approved, rejected, or prohibited by the City or Police Department.

To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data, which generates a downloadable ZIP file containing conversations in HTML format. For Claude, export is similar, except via “Settings > Privacy > Export Data”. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console, the Purview compliance center, or individual user account settings. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/ (reporting on AI-generated government documents obtained through public records requests in Everett, WA); see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files (collecting records of AI usage at the Texas Department of Transportation released in response to a public records request).

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

If you contend that any portion of the requested records is exempt from disclosure, please identify the specific legal basis for the withholding and release all reasonably segregable non-exempt material. If any part of this request is unclear, I am happy to discuss ways to clarify or narrow it while preserving the core records sought.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Jacqueline Xiong

The City is in receipt of your Public Records Act Request which you submitted on 4/8/2026. Your request was given the reference number R012215-040826 for tracking purposes. Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work. 

I request records related to the City of San Jose's employee use of generative AI tools, including ChatGPT Teams licenses, the city's generative AI guidance, and any related approval or tracking forms maintained by the City. 

We are requesting the following records: 
1. All interaction logs, prompts, inputs, outputs, conversation histories, and similar records created by employees using ChatGPT Teams, Wordly where it generated AI-assisted outputs for city work, and any other generative AI chatbot tools used by San Jose employees for the period from April 7, 2025 through the date this request is processed. Please provide these records in their native electronic format where available, including HTML exports, JSON, CSV, ZIP exports, or equivalent structured formats. 
2. Any administrative usage logs, dashboards, reports, or other records sufficient to show which employees, offices, or units had access to these tools and the volume of usage during the same period. 
3. Any policies, guidelines, training materials, approval documents, acceptable-use rules, or governance materials governing employee use of these tools or any other generative AI chatbot tools. 
4. Any records retention, deletion, preservation, or disposition policies applicable to these interaction logs or related AI-generated records. 

To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data, which generates a downloadable ZIP file containing conversations in HTML format. For Claude, export is similar, except via “Settings > Privacy > Export Data”. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console, the Purview compliance center, or individual user account settings. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful. 

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses). 

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/ (reporting on AI-generated government documents obtained through public records requests in Everett, WA); see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files (collecting records of AI usage at the Texas Department of Transportation released in response to a public records request). 

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20. 

If you contend that any portion of the requested records is exempt from disclosure, please identify the specific legal basis for the withholding and release all reasonably segregable non-exempt material. If any part of this request is unclear, I am happy to discuss ways to clarify or narrow it while preserving the core records sought. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Jacqueline Xiong 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2260903/ 
Our staff is currently searching for and collecting documents. We will notify you by 4/20/2026 of disclosable public records.  We will, at that time, also inform you of the costs incurred by the City in copying the responsive documents. 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. Again, thank you for using the Public Records Center. 
City of San Jose To monitor the progress or update this request please log into the Public Records Center

title: CPRA Request: Research Funding, DSP Operations, and Employment Records (UC Berkeley, 2022–2026) (Office Of The President (ucop), University Of California)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I request records reflecting research funding received by Professor Sheri L. Johnson at UC Berkeley from 2023 to 2026, including:

Total amounts and funding sources
Grant agreements or award documents
Budget summaries and expenditure categories (e.g., personnel, equipment, travel, overhead)
Any reports or documents reflecting allocation or use of such funds

I am not requesting personally identifiable information; aggregated or redacted records are sufficient.

I also request aggregated records (2023–2026) regarding the Disabled Students’ Program (DSP), including:

Total funding received (including government sources) and annual budgets
The number of accommodation requests, approvals, denials, and fulfillment rates
The number of complaints alleging failure to provide accommodations, including outcome categories
Aggregated, de-identified demographic breakdowns (e.g., age ranges, nationality categories), where available

I am not requesting personally identifiable student information; aggregated or redacted data will suffice.

I further request records related to DSP operations during the 2022–2023 labor action involving DSP advisors, including:

Communications, reports, or policy documents regarding service disruptions
Aggregated or de-identified complaint summaries related to DSP accommodation failures during this period

Additionally, I request employment-related records for Marilyn de la Cruz, including:

Employment contracts or offer letters
Job descriptions and role responsibilities
Salary ranges or compensation structure, where available

I am not requesting personally identifiable student information or confidential personnel evaluations; redacted or aggregated records will suffice.

Lastly,
I request records of communications between UCOP and UC Berkeley regarding:

The processing and handling of my CPRA requests
Administrative coordination related to my Government Claim and demand letter

This request excludes attorney-client privileged communications; however, I request that any non-privileged portions be disclosed, with redactions applied where necessary.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Young Jin Jung

Please provide these records in their native electronic format where available, including HTML exports, JSON, CSV, ZIP exports, or equivalent structured formats.
2. Any administrative usage logs, dashboards, reports, or other records sufficient to show which employees, offices, or units had access to these tools and the volume of usage during the same period.
3. Any policies, guidelines, training materials, approval documents, acceptable-use rules, or governance materials governing employee use of these tools or any other generative AI chatbot tools.
4. Any records retention, deletion, preservation, or disposition policies applicable to these interaction logs or related AI-generated records.

To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data, which generates a downloadable ZIP file containing conversations in HTML format. For Claude, export is similar, except via “Settings > Privacy > Export Data”. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console, the Purview compliance center, or individual user account settings. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/ (reporting on AI-generated government documents obtained through public records requests in Everett, WA); see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files (collecting records of AI usage at the Texas Department of Transportation released in response to a public records request).

Washington Secretary of State guidance issued in June 2024 states that generative AI prompts and outputs can qualify as public records and that retention depends on content and function, not format. That guidance is directly relevant here.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

If you contend that any portion of the requested records is exempt from disclosure, please identify the specific legal basis for the withholding and release all reasonably segregable non-exempt material. If any part of this request is unclear, I am happy to discuss ways to clarify or narrow it while preserving the core records sought.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jacqueline Xiong

title: Longitudinal Public Health Research (2016 - 2030)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Longitudinal Public Health Research (2016 - 2030)
communications.communication:
To FOIA Officer:

I am a researcher currently conducting a longitudinal study on the intersections of federal strategic planning and public health outcomes. Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, to support my analysis of the study, I am requesting the following public administrative records for the agency's last three Strategic Planning cycles.

1. Public Comment Records: Copies of written stakeholder feedback received during the formal input periods for these three Strategic Plan cycles:
• 2016 - 2020
• 2023 - 2026
• 2026 - 2030

2. Meeting/Hearing Minutes: Copies of the minutes or summary reports from any public hearings or townhall meetings held specifically to discuss the draft versions of these three Strategic Plans: 
• 2016 - 2020
• 2023 - 2026
• 2026 - 2030

3. Notice Documentation: Copies of the public notices (e.g., Federal Register Notices) used to announce the comment periods and hearings for these three Strategic Plans:
• 2016 - 2020
• 2023 - 2026
• 2026 - 2030

Fee Waiver:
I request that all fees be waived as this request is made for educational/scientific research purposes and is not for commercial use. To minimize administrative burden and costs, I am requesting all records be provided in digital format (e.g., PDF).

If the fees cannot be waived, please notify me before proceeding. I authorize up to $25 for any necessary research fees, though I anticipate these will be waived under my researcher status.

Processing & Delivery:
I am not seeking expedited processing, as this is part of a long-term comparative study. Please deliver the digital copies to the email address associated with this MuckRock request.

I look forward to your response within the statutory window of 20 business days.

Sincerely,
Ryan Miller

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Hello,

I am requesting records related to the installation, approval, and precise locations/addresses of ALL automatic license plate readers (ALPR) from Flock Safety/Flock Group, Inc.

Emails between city personnel, SPPD, and Flock Safety or  Flock Group, Inc. employees/domains (e.g., @flocksafety.com).
Including, but not limited to:
Akeia Dixon - installs@flocksafety.com
Dave@flocksafety.com
garrett@flocksafety.com
Do not include promo emails or emails from hello@flocksafety.com

All communications related to installations.
Keyword search:
"installations"
"install"
"technicians"
"public works"
"rebates"

Copies of attachments:
All documentation and records of installations that include their dates, locations, addresses, approvals by the city for installations, and all contracts related to the installations.
All contracts, licenses, MOUs, MOAs, data agreements, vendor agreements, letters of intent, Statement of Work documents, Scope of Work (SOW) documents, policy approvals, and all other documents with Flock Safety or  Flock Group, Inc.

Policies & Procedures. The Policies for Flock Safety use, data retention, sharing, and access.
Agreements allowing Flock Safety or  Flock Group, Inc. to share data with federal or any other agencies.
Names and positions of all individuals in the Santa Paula Police Department and/or City of Santa Paula that have the ability to access and search for any data collected by the Flock Safety ALPR's.

Limit the above searches from the period of 2018, to day this request is processed.

Audits:
1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of June 21, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of June 21, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

Please provide the audit data in a structured format such as .csv, .xls, or another machine-readable database filetype.

3. Data Sharing
a. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares ALPR data
b. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives ALPR data
c. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares “hot list” information
d. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives “hot list” information

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Anthony Jiminez

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: Connecticut FOIA Request – Town of Fairfield Planning & Zoning Records (2020–Present)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records from January 1, 2020 to present relating to Fairfield Planning & Zoning Department communications and draft materials, including:

1. Communications Involving Planning & Zoning Leadership
Emails to or from Emmeline Harrigan, including eharrigan@fairfieldct.org
Any additional Town email addresses used by Emmeline Harrigan
Any shared or group email accounts to which Emmeline Harrigan had access
Emails involving Jonathan Mullen
Emails involving the Director of Planning & Zoning (current and former)
Emails where the above individuals were sender, recipient, copied, or part of email threads

2. Commission Email Account (tpzcommission@fairfieldct.org)
Emails received by tpzcommission@fairfieldct.org
Emails forwarded to or from tpzcommission@fairfieldct.org
Internal communications discussing emails received by this account
Summaries, digests, or compilations of emails sent to the commission
Records reflecting how emails sent to tpzcommission@fairfieldct.org
 were reviewed, filtered, or distributed
Policies, procedures, or informal practices governing handling of this account

3. Draft Staff Reports and Internal Analyses
Draft staff reports
Draft memoranda or internal analyses
Tracked-changes or redlined versions
Email discussions regarding drafts or changes to recommendations
Draft calculations or revised analyses

4. Communications With Applicants and Developers
Emails between Planning & Zoning staff and applicants or property owners
Emails with developers or project sponsors
Emails with attorneys representing applicants or developers
Emails with engineers, architects, or consultants representing applicants
Pre-application discussions, informal guidance, or revisions to proposals
Communications with attorneys representing applicants or developers are included, as these involve external parties and are generally not subject to attorney-client privilege. This request does not seek privileged communications solely between the Town and its own legal counsel.

5. Consultant Communications
Emails with planning consultants
Emails with housing consultants
Emails with GIS consultants
Emails with traffic consultants
Emails with other land-use consultants
Drafts, analyses, or deliverables prepared by consultants

6. GIS and Calculation-Related Records
Emails and draft documents relating to GIS analyses
Zoning or density calculations
Affordable housing or 8-30g calculations
Protest petition calculations
Methodology discussions or revisions

7. Text Messages and Messaging Applications
Text messages, SMS, iMessage, or messaging application communications relating to the categories above
Messages to or from Emmeline Harrigan relating to Planning & Zoning matters
Messages involving tpzcommission@fairfieldct.org communications or distribution decisions
Messages involving draft staff reports, calculations, or communications with applicants, developers, or consultants

This request includes communications conducted on Town-issued devices, personal devices, personal email accounts, or messaging applications where such devices or accounts were used for Town business.

To assist in processing, responsive records may include those containing terms such as “draft,” “calculation,” “GIS,” “8-30g,” “affordable housing,” “consultant,” “staff report,” “application,” or “methodology.”

Electronic searches of email systems, shared mailboxes, and messaging platforms are acceptable. If any portion of this request is considered overly broad or burdensome, I am willing to work cooperatively to narrow the request.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

FairfieldResident

title: State Dept support for 2002 TV series "The American Embassy"

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: State Dept support for 2002 TV series "The American Embassy"
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to collaboration between the U.S. Department of State and the production of the 2002 TV series "The American Embassy," created by James D. Parriott. Such documents may include but should not be limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; photographs; and all records of communications between employees of the State Dept. and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "The American Embassy," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.
